old_claim.note: Electrical Lighting Systems provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Public electric street lighting depends on the lamp, wiring, switching, and distribution practices of electric lighting systems.

source_shape.source_locator: NPS states Edison invented not just an incandescent light but an electric lighting system with the elements necessary for practical lighting.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports lighting systems as the direct technical substrate for electric street lighting.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The scoped node is public electric lighting, so lighting-system capability is direct rather than optional municipal context.

ontology_before: Electric Street Lighting had an unsourced edge from Electrical Lighting Systems.
ontology_after: Electric Street Lighting retains the lighting-system edge with NPS source support.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: topology unchanged for this edge; evidence quality improved.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed to non-electric gas street lighting.
The node is broadened to urban lighting policy rather than electrical infrastructure.
A separate public_arc_lighting node should replace the current electrical_lighting_systems edge.
short_reason: Street lighting directly uses electric lighting systems.
